title: Check the RMS Screen for System Faults at the Start of a Support Call
summary: Use the RMS screen as the first diagnostic check during a support call to determine whether any system faults are currently shown and to establish an initial fact base before deeper analysis.
when_to_use: Use at the beginning of a support phone call when diagnosing a reported issue and you need to determine whether the system is currently showing faults in RMS.
not_for: Do not use this RMS check alone as a complete diagnosis or root-cause determination.
Do not use when RMS access is unavailable without noting that the condition could not be confirmed from available screens.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Open or obtain visibility to the RMS screen, check whether system faults are present, and document the observed fault state as the initial diagnostic baseline for the call.

safety_notes: This source describes the RMS fault review as an initial diagnostic check only.
Do not assume root cause from the RMS check alone.
access_or_tools_needed: Ability to view or receive reported system fault information during the support call

access_required: Access to the RMS screen or equivalent visibility to the RMS fault display during the support call
system_preconditions: A support call is in progress and the current system condition needs initial verification
RMS visibility is available directly or through reported screen information

visual_references.description: Training video frame discussing that the RMS screen should be the first check on a support call for system faults.
Overall System RMS page showing that system faults appear on the left and active AGV faults on the right.
Overall System RMS page reference noting where RMS content and system or AGV faults would appear and that the page may be blank if firewall access is blocked.

steps.title: Open the RMS screen first
steps.purpose: Start the support call with the source-designated first diagnostic check.
steps.instruction: At the start of the support call, go to the RMS screen before deeper troubleshooting.
steps.expected_result: The RMS screen is opened or its current content is made available for review.
steps.healthy_condition: The RMS screen is accessible and visible for inspection.
steps.failure_condition: The RMS screen cannot be accessed, is blank, or cannot be viewed from available support access.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Support guidance stating the RMS screen should be the first step on a phone call.
Overall System RMS page reference showing where system faults are viewed.
Overall System RMS page and note that the page may appear blank if firewall access is not open.

stop_or_escalate_if: RMS cannot be accessed or viewed from available support access.
The page is blank and the current condition cannot be confirmed from available screens.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: First thing first, go to that RMS screen, see if any system faults. This should always be your first step on a phone call.
Overall System RMS page ... shows system faults on the left and active AGV faults on the right.
This page should show RMS but appears blank because the firewall is not open.

steps.title: Check for system faults on RMS
steps.purpose: Determine whether the system is currently showing faults.
steps.instruction: Review the RMS screen and look for any system faults shown there.
steps.expected_result: You determine whether system faults are present on the RMS screen.
steps.healthy_condition: No system faults are shown, or the current fault state is clearly visible and readable.
steps.failure_condition: Fault visibility is unclear, the screen cannot be confirmed, or the displayed condition cannot be interpreted from available information.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System faults area on the left side of the Overall System RMS page.
System faults on the left and active AGV faults on the right.

stop_or_escalate_if: The RMS screen cannot be used to confirm whether faults are present.
The displayed condition is unavailable because RMS visibility is blocked.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Go to that RMS screen, see if any system faults.
The Overall System RMS page shows system faults on the left and active AGV faults on the right.
System faults appear on the left, AGV system faults on the right.

steps.title: Record the observed fault state
steps.purpose: Capture the initial observed condition from RMS without adding unsupported conclusions.
steps.instruction: Record whether faults are present and capture only the fault information that is visible on the RMS screen or directly reported from that screen.
steps.expected_result: The call notes include whether faults are present and what was observed from RMS.
steps.healthy_condition: Observed RMS fault state is documented clearly as seen or reported.
steps.failure_condition: The observed state cannot be documented because the screen is unavailable or the information cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Document visible fault state
actions.exact_action: Record whether faults are present and capture the observed fault information using only what is visible in the system or reported from the screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Support guidance emphasizing use of the RMS fault state as the first fact base on the call.

stop_or_escalate_if: You cannot confirm the reported condition from available screens.
The limitation in visibility prevents reliable documentation of the current fault state.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Record whether faults are present and capture the observed fault information using only what is visible in the system or reported from the screen.
The segment discusses documenting observations for later diagnosis.

steps.title: Use RMS fault state as the initial diagnostic baseline
steps.purpose: Anchor further troubleshooting to the initial observed RMS condition.
steps.instruction: Use the observed RMS fault state as the initial diagnostic baseline before attempting deeper analysis, and do not treat this check alone as a complete diagnosis.
steps.expected_result: Further troubleshooting starts from a confirmed initial RMS fault state.
steps.healthy_condition: The support call proceeds with a documented initial RMS condition and no unsupported root-cause assumptions.
steps.failure_condition: The RMS check is treated as a full diagnosis or root cause is assumed without further evidence.

actions.label: Set diagnostic baseline
actions.exact_action: Use the observed RMS fault state as the initial diagnostic baseline before attempting deeper analysis.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training guidance that this is the first step on a phone call, not the complete diagnosis.

stop_or_escalate_if: A root cause is being assumed from the RMS check alone.
No confirmable RMS baseline can be established from available access.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: This should always be your first step on a phone call.
Do not assume root cause from the RMS check alone; the source frames this as the first step, not a complete diagnosis.

success_criteria: The RMS screen is checked at the start of the support call.
It is established whether system faults are present on RMS.
The observed RMS fault state is documented as the initial diagnostic baseline.
healthy_conditions: The current system fault state can be clearly observed or reliably reported from the RMS screen.
The support user has an initial fact base for the call before deeper analysis.
failure_conditions: RMS cannot be accessed or is blank from available support access.
The reported condition cannot be confirmed from available screens.
The RMS check is used as a complete diagnosis instead of an initial diagnostic step.
escalation_guidance: If the RMS screen cannot be accessed or the reported condition cannot be confirmed from available screens, capture that limitation in notes.
If RMS visibility is blocked, note the access limitation rather than inferring system state.
If deeper diagnosis is needed, continue beyond this initial RMS check rather than assuming root cause.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Statement that the RMS screen should always be the first step on a phone call.
Overall System RMS page showing where system faults are viewed.
Overall System RMS page and note about blank display when firewall access is not open.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: First thing first, go to that RMS screen, see if any system faults. This should always be your first step on a phone call.

missing_details: The source does not provide exact RMS navigation steps.
The source does not provide specific fault field names or required note-taking fields.
The source does not define exact escalation targets after the initial RMS check.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this check.
